title: psilotum × intermedium
intro: Psilotum × intermedium, also known as the whisk fern, is a rare and ancient plant species that is a hybrid of the genera Psilotum and Tmesipteris. This plant is believed to have been around since the Devonian period, making it one of the oldest living plants on Earth. The whisk fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system.
